items.text: Harnesses, benchmarks, and evaluation frameworks for measuring agent quality and reliability.
### Benchmark Reality Check (real-world tool use)

items.name: MCPMark (paper)
items.description: 127-task MCP benchmark; reports best pass@1 at 52.56% (gpt-5-medium), with several strong models below 30% pass@1.

items.name: MCPMark (leaderboard)
items.description: Live model comparisons for realistic MCP task execution.

items.name: τ-bench
items.description: Tool-agent-user benchmark; reports strong function-calling agents still below 50% task success in its setup.

items.name: OSWorld
items.description: Open-ended computer-use benchmark; reports best model 12.24% vs 72.36% human success in initial results.

items.name: WebArena
items.description: Realistic web-task benchmark; reports best GPT-4-based agent at 14.41% vs 78.24% human.

items.name: GAIA
items.description: General assistant benchmark; original framing reports large human-model gap on tool-heavy questions.

items.name: AgentBench
items.description: Multi-domain benchmark suite for evaluating LLMs as agents.

items.name: AgentEvals
items.description: Evaluation utilities for scoring agent trajectories and outcomes.

items.name: AutoGen agbench
items.description: Benchmark runner for AutoGen agent workflows.

items.name: BrowserGym
items.description: Gym-style environment for training and evaluating browser agents.

items.name: browser-use
items.description: Framework for browser task automation and agent web interaction loops.

items.name: Inspect AI
items.description: Open-source framework for reproducible LLM and agent evaluations.

items.name: JailbreakBench
items.description: Open robustness benchmark for measuring jailbreak resistance in language models and agents.

items.name: MCPMark
items.description: Stress-testing benchmark for evaluating model and agent capability on MCP tasks.

items.name: MLE-bench
items.description: Benchmark harness for autonomous ML engineering tasks.

items.name: OSWorld
items.description: Open-ended benchmark environment for desktop computer-use agents.

items.name: OpenCUA
items.description: Open foundation stack for building and evaluating computer-use agents.

items.name: Stagehand
items.description: Browser automation framework for agentic web workflows and reproducible runs.

items.name: SWE-bench
items.description: Canonical benchmark for coding agents on real GitHub issue tasks.

items.name: Tau-Bench
items.description: Realistic interactive benchmark for measuring agent reliability.

items.name: WebArena
items.description: Real-world web task benchmark environment for browser agents.

items.name: WorkArena
items.description: Enterprise task benchmark for browser-based agent workflows.

items.name: AgentDojo
items.description: Security and robustness benchmark suite for tool-using agents.

items.name: AppWorld
items.description: Multi-application environment for benchmarking autonomous task completion.

items.name: AgentLab
items.description: Research platform for developing and evaluating web agents.

items.description: Interactive long-horizon benchmark environment for embodied planning agents.

items.text: Deep-dive reading map organized by the major categories in this repository.

items.name: ArXiv Deep Research Map (this repo)
items.description: Curated paper paths with per-category must-reads, a recent watchlist, and a monthly refresh workflow across frameworks, coding, MCP/tool use, eval reliability, memory, security, multimodal, quant, and on-chain/DeFi-adjacent research.

items.text: Systems-level context design: memory, retrieval, compression, routing, and long-horizon state management.
Focus here on *what information the model gets, when, and in what form*.

items.description: Patterns for long-horizon orchestration and reliability.

items.description: Practitioner lessons from building production autonomous workflows.

items.description: Key long-context failure analysis paper.

items.description: Practical context budget, memory, retrieval, and anti-drift checklist.

items.text: Neural memory, retrieval, and graph-linking foundations relevant to advanced agent cognition.

items.name: Neural Turing Machines (2014)
items.description: Foundational differentiable external-memory architecture.

items.name: End-to-End Memory Networks (2015)
items.description: Multi-hop memory lookup architecture for iterative reasoning.

items.name: Differentiable Neural Computer (2016)
items.description: Enhanced neural memory addressing for long-horizon reasoning.

items.name: Transformer-XL (2019)
items.description: Segment-level recurrence for long-context memory reuse.

items.name: Compressive Transformer (2019)
items.description: Compressed memory tiers for scalable sequence retention.

items.name: RAG (Lewis et al., 2020)
items.description: Canonical retrieval-augmented generation architecture.

items.description: Graph neural reasoning for multi-hop relational inference.

items.description: Practical guide for agent memory architectures and neural-symbolic linking patterns.
